title: Add CKC linked-media backend roundtrip panel proof
operator_request: Capture the remaining MT-010 proof gap: CKC linked-media panel actions should be verified against a backend/mock roundtrip, not only local fallback UI and pure request builders.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Resume by proving live panel actions against an in-process backend/mock that captures POST paths, JSON bodies, x-hsk-actor-id, returned rows, reload/refetch/new-panel reconstruction, and Argus-visible status/readouts. Do not treat local fallback, POST echo, or request-spec tests as full backend roundtrip proof.
state_recovery.closeout_dependency: MT-010 can move to ready-for-validation after focused local/Argus/request-spec checks, but CKC linked-media backend persistence must not be claimed complete until this MT proves backend-authority reload/refetch reconstruction.

scope.summary: Add a focused native panel/backend roundtrip proof for CKC media album create, media link, and image notes/tags save.
base_scope: Build or reuse an in-process TcpListener mock backend for the AtelierClient CKC media routes without adding new dependencies.
Load a CKC character with at least one album/media row through the client path, not the local fallback harness only.
Drive Argus click/set_value on album create, media link, and media notes/tags save from the Atelier CKC tab.
Assert captured backend requests include the exact POST routes, JSON bodies, and x-hsk-actor-id for the selected actor.
Return backend rows from the mock and assert the panel updates Argus-visible album/media/readout/status nodes from those rows.

After create/link/save, force a reload/refetch/new-panel path and assert albums, linked media, notes, tags, actor-visible status, and selected refs reconstruct from backend authority rather than in-memory state.
Document the proof in the internal manual or MT closeout so future models know this is the backend-authority proof path.
acceptance_criteria: The test drives the production AtelierPanel with an AtelierClient, not only a no-client local fallback harness.
The mock backend captures album create, album link, and media notes/tags save requests with correct paths, bodies, and actor headers.
The mock backend responses are parsed and reflected in Argus-visible CKC album/media/status/readout nodes.
The proof reloads/refetches/rebuilds the panel after write operations and verifies the same album/media/notes/tags state comes back from backend authority.
The proof distinguishes backend:persistent from local-preview:not-persisted and does not overclaim local fallback persistence.
The test is focused and runnable without launching the foreground Handshake GUI.
risks_and_controls: Risk: a mock can drift from backend response shape. Control: use existing parser DTO shapes and include route/body/header assertions.
Risk: the panel passes from POST echo or in-memory state without persistence. Control: require reload/refetch/new-panel reconstruction from backend-returned authority state after the write sequence.
Risk: the test becomes slow or flaky. Control: use bounded TcpListener timeouts and a narrow route sequence.
Risk: panel starts on the wrong tab. Control: steer the tab through Argus or set the panel active tab before proof, then assert CKC author_ids are visible.
verification: Argus snapshot assertions after backend responses.

manual_requirement: Required if proof path changes operator/model workflow.

handoff.note: Next active recovery point after MT-010 reached ready-for-validation. Implementation has not yet started beyond this contract/refinement. Created during MT-010 closeout after adversarial review found that local fallback and request-spec tests are not a full panel/backend roundtrip proof. This MT is the backend-authority proof gate for CKC linked-media persistence before MT-038 or WP closeout can claim the workflow is proven end to end.
